{"step_by_step_thinking": "Next generation sequencing involves sequencing DNA or RNA directly from a sample, but it is not primarily an RNA-based technique. PCR (polymerase chain reaction) is used to amplify DNA or RNA, but it is not primarily an RNA-based technique. Sanger's technique is a DNA sequencing method that does not involve RNA. Western blotting is a technique used to detect proteins, not RNA. However, PCR can be used to amplify RNA, making it a primarily RNA-based technique in certain contexts.", "answer_choice": "B"}
